summary: All iteration 12 features tested and verified. Wikidata-based human verification for search (P31=Q5 filter) is working correctly - 'gucci' returns only humans (Gucci Mane, Maurizio Gucci, etc.), 'lion' returns only Alfred Lion (no animal), 'beyonce' returns Beyoncé, 'taylor' returns Taylor Swift, Elizabeth Taylor, etc. Price history tracking is fully functional - API endpoints work, price history is recorded when searching, and frontend modal displays correctly with current price, tier, and history entries.
passed_tests: Search 'gucci' returns only humans (Gucci Mane, Maurizio Gucci, Guccio Gucci, Paolo Gucci, Aldo Gucci) - NO BRAND
Search 'lion' returns only humans (Alfred Lion) - NO ANIMAL
Search 'beyonce' returns Beyoncé (human verified via Wikidata) with tier A and price
Search 'taylor' returns Taylor Swift, Elizabeth Taylor, Angus Taylor, Teyana Taylor, Christine Taylor - ALL HUMANS
Price history API endpoint GET /api/price-history/celebrity-name/{name} works correctly
Price history API endpoint GET /api/celebrity/{id}/price-history works correctly
Price history returns 404 for non-existent celebrities
Price history is recorded when searching for celebrities
Price history button (chart icon) appears on celebrity cards (top-right)
Price history modal displays correctly when button is clicked
Price history modal shows current price, tier, and history entries
Search 'nike' returns only humans - NO BRAND
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 12 features verified: (1) Wikidata-based human verification for search using SPARQL query to check P31=Q5 (instance of human). (2) Price history stored in MongoDB 'price_history' collection with celebrity_id, price, tier, buzz_score, recorded_at fields. (3) Frontend modal displays history with trend indicators. Search filtering correctly excludes brands (Gucci, Nike), animals (Lion), and non-human entities.
